checklist.source_text: A full- or part-time employee in California who has been employed for a total of at least 12 months (52 weeks) with the employer at any time prior to the commencement of a CFRA leave
checklist.plain_notes: Employment periods prior to a break in service of seven years or more need not be counted, except for breaks caused by military service obligation or written agreement.

checklist.plain_notes: Hours must be actually worked within the meaning of the California Labor Code and Industrial Welfare Commission Wage Orders during the 12-month period immediately prior to the date the CFRA leave is to commence.

checklist.name: pregnancy_disability_leave
checklist.label: pregnancy disability leave
checklist.plain_notes: Includes disability because of pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition. Examples in the regulation include severe morning sickness, prenatal or postnatal care, bed rest, gestational diabetes, pregnancy-induced hypertension, preeclampsia, post-partum depression, childbirth, loss or end of pregnancy, and recovery from childbirth, loss, or end of pregnancy, but the list is non-exclusive.

checklist.plain_notes: There is no requirement that either the employee or child have a serious health condition. There is also no requirement that the employee no longer be disabled by pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition before taking CFRA leave for birth of the child.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: A full- or part-time employee in California who has been employed for a total of at least 12 months (52 weeks) with the employer at any time prior to the commencement of a CFRA leave
checklist.plain_notes: Employment periods prior to a break in service of seven years or more need not be counted, except for breaks caused by military service obligation or written agreement.

checklist.name: hours_worked_last_12_months
checklist.label: hours worked last 12 months
checklist.source_text: who has actually worked (within the meaning of the California Labor Code and Industrial Welfare Commission Wage Orders) for the employer at least 1,250 hours during the 12-month period immediately prior to the date the CFRA leave is to commence.
checklist.plain_notes: Hours must be actually worked within the meaning of the California Labor Code and Industrial Welfare Commission Wage Orders during the 12-month period immediately prior to the date the CFRA leave is to commence.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment with an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A))
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to salaried employees seeking paid parental leave; rehired retirees receiving annuity payments during first 1,040 hours of the calendar year are not eligible.

checklist.name: hours_worked_preceding_6_months
checklist.label: hours worked preceding 6 months
checklist.source_text: if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours for an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A)) in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to hourly employees seeking paid parental leave; the 700 hours must be worked in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

checklist.name: leave_request_approval_required
checklist.label: leave request approval required
checklist.source_text: Employees are expected to properly request and receive approval for absence from work.
checklist.plain_notes: Employees must follow agency procedures to request and receive approval for absence.

checklist.name: leave_extension_request_timing
checklist.label: leave extension request timing
checklist.source_text: employee must request it in writing from her/his supervisor prior to the leave expiration or adhere to other agency procedures for timely requesting an extension
checklist.plain_notes: Extension must be requested in writing before leave expires or per agency procedures.

checklist.name: periodic_status_reports_uncertain_leave
checklist.label: periodic status reports uncertain leave
checklist.source_text: The agency may require an employee on leave with an uncertain end date to provide periodic reports during the leave regarding the employee's status and intent to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require periodic reports during leave with uncertain end date.

checklist.name: sick_leave_substitution_for_annual_leave_deadline
checklist.label: sick leave substitution for annual leave deadline
checklist.source_text: A request for substitution of sick leave for annual leave must be made to the agency within two (2) weeks after the employee has returned to duty.
checklist.plain_notes: Request to substitute sick leave for annual leave must be made within 2 weeks of return to duty.

checklist.name: parental_leave_employer_notice_to_employees
checklist.label: parental leave employer notice to employees
checklist.source_text: Agencies must provide notice of paid parental leave benefits to all employees anticipated to become eligible for paid parental leave upon hire and annually thereafter.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies must notify all employees anticipated to become eligible upon hire and annually.

checklist.name: return_to_work_medical_release
checklist.label: return to work medical release
checklist.source_text: Each agency may, as a condition of return, require an employee who is absent from work because of illness or injury to supply an appropriate medical release or certification that the employee is able to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require medical release or certification confirming ability to perform essential functions upon return from illness or injury.

checklist.name: fitness_for_duty_intermittent_fmla
checklist.label: fitness for duty intermittent fmla
checklist.source_text: An agency may require fitness-for-duty certification only if the agency reasonably believes the return could pose significant risk of harm to the employee or others. Such certification may be required no more often than every 30 calendar days.
checklist.plain_notes: Fitness-for-duty certification may be required only if the agency reasonably believes return could pose significant risk of harm, and may be required no more often than every 30 calendar days.

checklist.name: sick_leave_evidence_requirement
checklist.label: sick leave evidence requirement
checklist.source_text: An employee may be required to furnish evidence to support the use of sick leave if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require supporting evidence if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.

checklist.name: sick_leave_illness_during_annual_leave
checklist.label: sick leave illness during annual leave
checklist.source_text: If an employee is ill for three (3) workdays or more during a period of annual leave, the period of illness may be charged to sick leave if the employee provides satisfactory written evidence supporting the illness during annual leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Satisfactory written evidence is required to charge illness during annual leave to sick leave.

checklist.name: divested_sick_leave_restoration_documentation
checklist.label: divested sick leave restoration documentation
checklist.source_text: To obtain restoration of divested sick leave, an employee must apply in writing to the employing agency and include supporting documentation.
checklist.plain_notes: Employee must apply in writing and include supporting documentation to obtain restoration of divested sick leave.

notes: relationship/included_relationships: relationship='self' IN [['self'], ['parent'], ['spouse'], ['child']] → True

checklist.name: included_relationships
checklist.label: relationship
checklist.plain_notes: Georgia sick leave covers the employee's own illness, injury, disability, dental or medical care, exposure to contagious disease, and illness, injury, or disability in the employee's immediate family requiring the employee's presence. Immediate family is broader in the regulation, but this GT uses only the project's predefined relationship tags.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment with an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A))
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to salaried employees seeking paid parental leave; rehired retirees receiving annuity payments during first 1,040 hours of the calendar year are not eligible.

checklist.name: hours_worked_preceding_6_months
checklist.label: hours worked preceding 6 months
checklist.source_text: if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours for an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A)) in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to hourly employees seeking paid parental leave; the 700 hours must be worked in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

checklist.name: leave_request_approval_required
checklist.label: leave request approval required
checklist.source_text: Employees are expected to properly request and receive approval for absence from work.
checklist.plain_notes: Employees must follow agency procedures to request and receive approval for absence.

checklist.name: leave_extension_request_timing
checklist.label: leave extension request timing
checklist.source_text: employee must request it in writing from her/his supervisor prior to the leave expiration or adhere to other agency procedures for timely requesting an extension
checklist.plain_notes: Extension must be requested in writing before leave expires or per agency procedures.

checklist.name: periodic_status_reports_uncertain_leave
checklist.label: periodic status reports uncertain leave
checklist.source_text: The agency may require an employee on leave with an uncertain end date to provide periodic reports during the leave regarding the employee's status and intent to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require periodic reports during leave with uncertain end date.

checklist.name: sick_leave_substitution_for_annual_leave_deadline
checklist.label: sick leave substitution for annual leave deadline
checklist.source_text: A request for substitution of sick leave for annual leave must be made to the agency within two (2) weeks after the employee has returned to duty.
checklist.plain_notes: Request to substitute sick leave for annual leave must be made within 2 weeks of return to duty.

checklist.name: parental_leave_employer_notice_to_employees
checklist.label: parental leave employer notice to employees
checklist.source_text: Agencies must provide notice of paid parental leave benefits to all employees anticipated to become eligible for paid parental leave upon hire and annually thereafter.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies must notify all employees anticipated to become eligible upon hire and annually.

checklist.name: return_to_work_medical_release
checklist.label: return to work medical release
checklist.source_text: Each agency may, as a condition of return, require an employee who is absent from work because of illness or injury to supply an appropriate medical release or certification that the employee is able to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require medical release or certification confirming ability to perform essential functions upon return from illness or injury.

checklist.name: fitness_for_duty_intermittent_fmla
checklist.label: fitness for duty intermittent fmla
checklist.source_text: An agency may require fitness-for-duty certification only if the agency reasonably believes the return could pose significant risk of harm to the employee or others. Such certification may be required no more often than every 30 calendar days.
checklist.plain_notes: Fitness-for-duty certification may be required only if the agency reasonably believes return could pose significant risk of harm, and may be required no more often than every 30 calendar days.

checklist.name: sick_leave_evidence_requirement
checklist.label: sick leave evidence requirement
checklist.source_text: An employee may be required to furnish evidence to support the use of sick leave if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require supporting evidence if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.

checklist.name: sick_leave_illness_during_annual_leave
checklist.label: sick leave illness during annual leave
checklist.source_text: If an employee is ill for three (3) workdays or more during a period of annual leave, the period of illness may be charged to sick leave if the employee provides satisfactory written evidence supporting the illness during annual leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Satisfactory written evidence is required to charge illness during annual leave to sick leave.

checklist.name: divested_sick_leave_restoration_documentation
checklist.label: divested sick leave restoration documentation
checklist.source_text: To obtain restoration of divested sick leave, an employee must apply in writing to the employing agency and include supporting documentation.
checklist.plain_notes: Employee must apply in writing and include supporting documentation to obtain restoration of divested sick leave.

notes: relationship/included_relationships: relationship='self' IN [['self'], ['parent'], ['spouse'], ['child']] → True

checklist.name: included_relationships
checklist.label: relationship
checklist.plain_notes: Georgia sick leave covers the employee's own illness, injury, disability, dental or medical care, exposure to contagious disease, and illness, injury, or disability in the employee's immediate family requiring the employee's presence. Immediate family is broader in the regulation, but this GT uses only the project's predefined relationship tags.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment with an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A))
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to salaried employees seeking paid parental leave; rehired retirees receiving annuity payments during first 1,040 hours of the calendar year are not eligible.

checklist.name: hours_worked_preceding_6_months
checklist.label: hours worked preceding 6 months
checklist.source_text: if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours for an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A)) in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to hourly employees seeking paid parental leave; the 700 hours must be worked in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

checklist.name: leave_request_approval_required
checklist.label: leave request approval required
checklist.source_text: Employees are expected to properly request and receive approval for absence from work.
checklist.plain_notes: Employees must follow agency procedures to request and receive approval for absence.

checklist.name: leave_extension_request_timing
checklist.label: leave extension request timing
checklist.source_text: employee must request it in writing from her/his supervisor prior to the leave expiration or adhere to other agency procedures for timely requesting an extension
checklist.plain_notes: Extension must be requested in writing before leave expires or per agency procedures.

checklist.name: periodic_status_reports_uncertain_leave
checklist.label: periodic status reports uncertain leave
checklist.source_text: The agency may require an employee on leave with an uncertain end date to provide periodic reports during the leave regarding the employee's status and intent to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require periodic reports during leave with uncertain end date.

checklist.name: sick_leave_substitution_for_annual_leave_deadline
checklist.label: sick leave substitution for annual leave deadline
checklist.source_text: A request for substitution of sick leave for annual leave must be made to the agency within two (2) weeks after the employee has returned to duty.
checklist.plain_notes: Request to substitute sick leave for annual leave must be made within 2 weeks of return to duty.

checklist.name: parental_leave_employer_notice_to_employees
checklist.label: parental leave employer notice to employees
checklist.source_text: Agencies must provide notice of paid parental leave benefits to all employees anticipated to become eligible for paid parental leave upon hire and annually thereafter.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies must notify all employees anticipated to become eligible upon hire and annually.

checklist.name: sick_leave_evidence_requirement
checklist.label: sick leave evidence requirement
checklist.source_text: An employee may be required to furnish evidence to support the use of sick leave if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require supporting evidence if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.

checklist.name: sick_leave_illness_during_annual_leave
checklist.label: sick leave illness during annual leave
checklist.source_text: If an employee is ill for three (3) workdays or more during a period of annual leave, the period of illness may be charged to sick leave if the employee provides satisfactory written evidence supporting the illness during annual leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Satisfactory written evidence is required to charge illness during annual leave to sick leave.

checklist.name: divested_sick_leave_restoration_documentation
checklist.label: divested sick leave restoration documentation
checklist.source_text: To obtain restoration of divested sick leave, an employee must apply in writing to the employing agency and include supporting documentation.
checklist.plain_notes: Employee must apply in writing and include supporting documentation to obtain restoration of divested sick leave.

checklist.name: paid_parental_leave_supporting_documentation
checklist.label: paid parental leave supporting documentation
checklist.source_text: Agencies may require employees to submit appropriate supporting documentation for the use of paid parental leave. Any required supporting documentation shall be the same as that required for the use of federal family and medical leave under Section (7) of Rule 478-1-.23, Family and Medical Leave, for the same qualifying event.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies may require supporting documentation for paid parental leave. Required documentation must be the same as that required for federal FMLA for the same qualifying event.

checklist.name: included_relationships
checklist.label: relationship
checklist.plain_notes: Georgia paid parental leave covers birth of the employee's child. Pregnancy-related illness, injury, or disability is not separately stated in the paid parental leave section; if relevant, it should be handled under the health condition sick leave logic.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment with an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A))
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to salaried employees seeking paid parental leave; rehired retirees receiving annuity payments during first 1,040 hours of the calendar year are not eligible.

checklist.name: hours_worked_preceding_6_months
checklist.label: hours worked preceding 6 months
checklist.source_text: if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours for an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A)) in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to hourly employees seeking paid parental leave; the 700 hours must be worked in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

checklist.name: leave_request_approval_required
checklist.label: leave request approval required
checklist.source_text: Employees are expected to properly request and receive approval for absence from work.
checklist.plain_notes: Employees must follow agency procedures to request and receive approval for absence.

checklist.name: leave_extension_request_timing
checklist.label: leave extension request timing
checklist.source_text: employee must request it in writing from her/his supervisor prior to the leave expiration or adhere to other agency procedures for timely requesting an extension
checklist.plain_notes: Extension must be requested in writing before leave expires or per agency procedures.

checklist.name: periodic_status_reports_uncertain_leave
checklist.label: periodic status reports uncertain leave
checklist.source_text: The agency may require an employee on leave with an uncertain end date to provide periodic reports during the leave regarding the employee's status and intent to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require periodic reports during leave with uncertain end date.

checklist.name: sick_leave_substitution_for_annual_leave_deadline
checklist.label: sick leave substitution for annual leave deadline
checklist.source_text: A request for substitution of sick leave for annual leave must be made to the agency within two (2) weeks after the employee has returned to duty.
checklist.plain_notes: Request to substitute sick leave for annual leave must be made within 2 weeks of return to duty.

checklist.name: parental_leave_employer_notice_to_employees
checklist.label: parental leave employer notice to employees
checklist.source_text: Agencies must provide notice of paid parental leave benefits to all employees anticipated to become eligible for paid parental leave upon hire and annually thereafter.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies must notify all employees anticipated to become eligible upon hire and annually.

checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require supporting evidence if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.

checklist.name: sick_leave_illness_during_annual_leave
checklist.label: sick leave illness during annual leave
checklist.source_text: If an employee is ill for three (3) workdays or more during a period of annual leave, the period of illness may be charged to sick leave if the employee provides satisfactory written evidence supporting the illness during annual leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Satisfactory written evidence is required to charge illness during annual leave to sick leave.

checklist.name: divested_sick_leave_restoration_documentation

checklist.plain_notes: Employee must apply in writing and include supporting documentation to obtain restoration of divested sick leave.

checklist.plain_notes: Agencies may require supporting documentation for paid parental leave. Required documentation must be the same as that required for federal FMLA for the same qualifying event.

checklist.name: employment_duration_months
checklist.label: employment duration months
checklist.source_text: if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment with an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A))
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to salaried employees seeking paid parental leave; rehired retirees receiving annuity payments during first 1,040 hours of the calendar year are not eligible.

checklist.name: hours_worked_preceding_6_months
checklist.label: hours worked preceding 6 months
checklist.source_text: if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours for an employing entity (as defined in O.C.G.A. 45-20-17(a)(2)(A)) in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.
checklist.plain_notes: Applies only to hourly employees seeking paid parental leave; the 700 hours must be worked in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

checklist.name: leave_request_approval_required
checklist.label: leave request approval required
checklist.source_text: Employees are expected to properly request and receive approval for absence from work.
checklist.plain_notes: Employees must follow agency procedures to request and receive approval for absence.

checklist.name: leave_extension_request_timing
checklist.label: leave extension request timing
checklist.source_text: employee must request it in writing from her/his supervisor prior to the leave expiration or adhere to other agency procedures for timely requesting an extension
checklist.plain_notes: Extension must be requested in writing before leave expires or per agency procedures.

checklist.name: periodic_status_reports_uncertain_leave
checklist.label: periodic status reports uncertain leave
checklist.source_text: The agency may require an employee on leave with an uncertain end date to provide periodic reports during the leave regarding the employee's status and intent to return to work.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require periodic reports during leave with uncertain end date.

checklist.name: sick_leave_substitution_for_annual_leave_deadline
checklist.label: sick leave substitution for annual leave deadline
checklist.source_text: A request for substitution of sick leave for annual leave must be made to the agency within two (2) weeks after the employee has returned to duty.
checklist.plain_notes: Request to substitute sick leave for annual leave must be made within 2 weeks of return to duty.

checklist.name: parental_leave_employer_notice_to_employees
checklist.label: parental leave employer notice to employees
checklist.source_text: Agencies must provide notice of paid parental leave benefits to all employees anticipated to become eligible for paid parental leave upon hire and annually thereafter.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies must notify all employees anticipated to become eligible upon hire and annually.

checklist.name: sick_leave_evidence_requirement
checklist.label: sick leave evidence requirement
checklist.source_text: An employee may be required to furnish evidence to support the use of sick leave if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Agency may require supporting evidence if the employee uses 17 or more hours of sick leave in a 30 calendar day period or has demonstrated excessive or abusive use of sick leave.

checklist.name: sick_leave_illness_during_annual_leave
checklist.label: sick leave illness during annual leave
checklist.source_text: If an employee is ill for three (3) workdays or more during a period of annual leave, the period of illness may be charged to sick leave if the employee provides satisfactory written evidence supporting the illness during annual leave.
checklist.plain_notes: Satisfactory written evidence is required to charge illness during annual leave to sick leave.

checklist.name: divested_sick_leave_restoration_documentation
checklist.label: divested sick leave restoration documentation
checklist.source_text: To obtain restoration of divested sick leave, an employee must apply in writing to the employing agency and include supporting documentation.
checklist.plain_notes: Employee must apply in writing and include supporting documentation to obtain restoration of divested sick leave.

checklist.name: paid_parental_leave_supporting_documentation
checklist.label: paid parental leave supporting documentation
checklist.source_text: Agencies may require employees to submit appropriate supporting documentation for the use of paid parental leave. Any required supporting documentation shall be the same as that required for the use of federal family and medical leave under Section (7) of Rule 478-1-.23, Family and Medical Leave, for the same qualifying event.
checklist.plain_notes: Agencies may require supporting documentation for paid parental leave. Required documentation must be the same as that required for federal FMLA for the same qualifying event.
